title: Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Moroccan dish featuring tender lamb in a rich and flavorful sauce, made with chilled butter and cooked in a papillote, perfect for a crowd and suitable for a Buddhist temple gathering
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit
In a large bowl, mix together the lamb, olive oil, onion, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, and cinnamon
Season with salt and pepper to taste
Transfer the lamb mixture to a large piece of parchment paper or foil, and dot the top with chilled butter
Fold the parchment paper or foil over the lamb to create a papillote, and seal the edges
Place the papillote in a large Dutch oven or heavy pot with a tight-fitting lid
Add the dried apricots to the pot, and pour in enough water to come halfway up the side of the papillote
Cover the pot, and bring the water to a boil
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven, and cook for 2.5 hours
Remove the pot from the oven, and let it cool slightly before serving
